text:
Appart from light microscopes, scientists also use powerful electron microscopes that reveal the fine details of a cell structure. These instruments use a beam of electrons instead of light to produce highly magnified images, allowing us to see cell structure at the nanometre scale with remarkable clarity (a nanometre is one-billionth of a metre).

Estimated size of the onion peel cell = 

Suppose, 25 cells are seen along the diameter. In that case, the size of one onion cell would be 5000 µm/25 = 200 µm.

If the estimated size of an onion peel cell is 200 µm, how much does a light microscope magnify this cell? The total magnification of a microscope depends on its magnifying power of the eyepiece and the objective lens. If both the eyepiece and the objective lens have the magnifying power of 10X, then the total magnification will be 100X. This means that a cell with an estimated size of 200 µm will appear 100 times larger.

Thus, a microscope allows us to see very small structures clearly and is an essential tool for studying the cell structure. Over the years, scientists have improved the microscope by improving its three main features—resolution (measure of clarity), contrast (the difference in brightness between various

diagrams.label: Structure of a light microscope
diagrams.description: A side view diagram of a light microscope. The main components are labeled: The Coarse adjustment knob is located at the top left. Below it is the Fine adjustment knob. The Body tube is the main vertical tube. The Objective lens is attached to the body tube. The Stage is the flat platform where the slide rests. Below the stage is the Mirror. The Base provides the support, and the Arm connects the base to the body tube.

diagrams.label: An electron microscope
diagrams.description: A side view diagram of an electron microscope. The labeled parts include the Objective lens at the front, the Stage platform, and the Base support.
